messages.subject: [R-sig-DCM] Incorporating a "None" or constant alternative in a stated choice experiment
messages.subject_clean: Incorporating a "None" or constant alternative in a stated choice experiment
messages.body_plain:
Good morning,

Does anyone have any experience incorporating a None alternative in a 
stated choice experiment.  I'm particularly interested in how it's 
handled in the estimation.  Since the design matrix can't include a None 
(there's nothing to design), how do people add it at estimation.  I 
merely add a constant row to each alternative list with the word "None" 
as the level and, if it's part of the experiment, 0 for price.  The SAS 
online manuals by Warren Kuhfeld show it being handled this way.  Any 
opinions/experience other ways to deal with it?

Thanks,

Walt

________________________

Walter R. Paczkowski, Ph.D.

Plainsboro, NJ 08536

messages.subject: [R-sig-DCM] segmenting consumers after a dcm
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

I just completed a dcm (not a conjoint) for a client.  Now they would 
like to segment the consumers based on utilities from the model, 
claiming that they had this done for them before although they don't 
know what or how it was done.  My model had coefficients estimated 
across all consumers, not for each one separately.  I don't even think 
that makes sense to do for each individual.  Has anyone ever done 
segmentation after a dcm?

Thanks,

Walt
